words.definition: One complete movement of an exercise, commonly called a rep
words.example: Do three sets of twelve repetitions for each exercise.

words.definition: Physical exercise that raises your heart rate and improves cardiovascular fitness
words.example: I do thirty minutes of cardio before lifting weights each day.

words.definition: The way in which you hold your body when standing, sitting, or moving
words.example: Good posture helps prevent chronic back and neck pain.

words.definition: A fixed set of exercises performed regularly in a planned order
words.example: Sticking to a consistent workout routine is key to achieving results.

words.definition: Light exercise done before intense training to prepare the body and prevent injury
words.example: Always do a five-minute warm-up before starting your main workout.

words.definition: The ability to sustain prolonged physical or mental effort without tiring
words.example: Running long distances requires considerable stamina and mental focus.

words.definition: The ability to move joints and muscles through their full range of motion
words.example: Regular yoga practice improves your strength, balance, and flexibility.

words.definition: The degree of effort, force, or energy applied during a workout
words.example: Gradually increase the intensity of your workouts to avoid injury.

words.definition: The period of rest and repair that the body needs after intense exercise
words.example: Proper nutrition and sleep are essential for muscle recovery.

words.definition: A force that opposes movement, used to build muscle strength in training
words.example: Resistance training with weights helps build muscle mass and bone density.

words.definition: The ability to perform sustained exercise over a long period of time
words.example: Cycling and swimming are excellent activities for building endurance.

words.definition: A person who assists and watches over someone lifting heavy weights for safety
words.example: Always have a spotter nearby when attempting a new personal best.

words.definition: The chemical processes in your body that convert food and drink into energy
words.example: Regular exercise helps speed up your metabolism over time.

words.definition: A period during training when progress stops despite continued effort
words.example: I hit a plateau and had to change my routine to keep seeing progress.

words.definition: Exercising too frequently or intensely without adequate rest, causing fatigue or injury
words.example: Overtraining can lead to burnout, injury, and decreased performance.
